heading: Arto intake batch 001 formalizes contra-cyclic and QA geometry sources as a controlled prior-art lane
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, arto-heino, contra-cyclic, external-prior-art]
body: Built `arto_intake_batch_001.json` and `build_arto_intake_batch_001.py` as the first controlled Arto Heino intake packet for the Pyth/QA/RT subproject. The batch records 5 sources with explicit witness status instead of mixing live reads and guesses: 3 stable live surfaces (`Contra-Cyclic Harmonic Archimedean Geometry`, `tag/geometry`, and `tag/quantum-arithmetic`), 1 follow-up surface preserved from the live browse pass (`tag/ben-iverson`), and 1 queued GitHub profile target (`github.com/arto-heino`). The contra-cyclic post is now isolated as a high-priority mechanics lane linking Archimedes, Ben Iverson QA, toroids, pendulums, versins, and dynamic machine geometry. The QA tag surface is explicitly useful for quantum-ellipse and Gaussian-integer terminology tracking, and the geometry tag surface is useful for Saqqara/Ostrakon ellipse archaeology. Honest verdict: this is an intake stabilization step, not yet a reinterpretation result, but it converts the Arto pull from ad hoc browsing into a reproducible, source-graded backlog.
